title: cerastium quaternellum subsp. octandrum
intro: Cerastium quaternellum subsp. octandrum, also known as a type of chickweed, is a low-growing, creeping plant that belongs to the carnation family and is native to Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Cerastium quaternellum subsp. octandrum include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support digestive health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Cerastium quaternellum subsp. octandrum include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and digestive problems.
